title: Question of Esperet and Raymond on improvability of expansion bounds
statement_text: Can either of the bounds $\max\!\left(\tfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}-1,\,0\right)\leq b_\varepsilon\leq\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}-1$ and $B_\varepsilon\leq\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}-1$ be improved (in particular, in terms of the multiplicative constants in the exponents)?
context_text: Corollary 2 (derived from Plotkin–Rao–Smith) and Theorem 5 (Esperet–Raymond) together yield the bounds on $b_\varepsilon$ and $B_\varepsilon$, the extreme exponents governing the expansion of hereditary graph classes whose balanced-separator function satisfies $s_\mathcal{G}(n)=\Theta(n^{1-\varepsilon})$. Esperet and Raymond asked whether either bound could be substantially improved, suggesting that $d$-dimensional grids might provide insight.
paper_contribution: Shows that neither exponent can be substantially improved: $B_\varepsilon=\frac{1}{\varepsilon}-1$ exactly (matching the upper bound), $b_\varepsilon=0$ for $\frac{1}{2}\leq\varepsilon\leq 1$, and $b_\varepsilon\leq\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-\frac{1}{2}$ for $0<\varepsilon<\frac{1}{2}$, leaving a gap of at most $\frac{1}{2}$ above the lower bound $\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-1$.
notes: The original question is not stated in a formal labelled environment in this paper; reconstructed from prose. PDF source.
paper_title: A note on sublinear separators and expansion

title: Informal open question on the exact value of $b_\varepsilon$
statement_text: It is unclear whether the upper or the lower bound on $b_\varepsilon$ can be improved for $0<\varepsilon<\frac{1}{2}$. While the fact that $b_{1/2}=0$ matches the lower bound suggests that a better construction improving the upper bound in general could exist, it is also plausible that this is just a "dimension 2" artifact and in fact the lower bound might be possible to improve for $\varepsilon<1/2$ (possibly leading to discontinuity of $b_\varepsilon$ at $\varepsilon=1/2$).
context_text: After establishing $\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-1\leq b_\varepsilon\leq\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-\frac{1}{2}$ for $0<\varepsilon<\frac{1}{2}$ and $b_\varepsilon=0$ for $\frac{1}{2}\leq\varepsilon\leq 1$, the paper notes that the remaining gap of $\frac{1}{2}$ in the bounds on $b_\varepsilon$ is unresolved, and that the behaviour at $\varepsilon=1/2$ makes the problem structurally interesting.
notes: Stated as an open remark in the concluding summary. PDF source.
paper_title: A note on sublinear separators and expansion

title: Informal conjecture $b'_\varepsilon = b_\varepsilon$ for $0<\varepsilon<\tfrac{1}{2}$
statement_text: It seems likely that $b'_\varepsilon = b_\varepsilon$ when $0 < \varepsilon < \frac{1}{2}$ as well.
context_text: The parameter $b'_\varepsilon$ is defined analogously to $b_\varepsilon$ but with the one-sided condition $s_\mathcal{G}(n)=\Omega(n^{1-\varepsilon})$ rather than $\Theta(n^{1-\varepsilon})$; equality $b'_\varepsilon=b_\varepsilon=0$ already holds for $\frac{1}{2}\leq\varepsilon\leq 1$, but extending this to $0<\varepsilon<\frac{1}{2}$ requires showing that a one-sided lower bound on separators alone forces the same expansion lower bound, which is not obvious.
notes: Conjectural language 'It seems likely' appears in the final section. PDF source.
paper_title: A note on sublinear separators and expansion
